Epic: E01 - Product foundation and ALM
Title: As a solution administrator, I want to install CCSB as a managed solution with required roles and environment settings so that the product is governed through standard ALM.
AcceptanceOutcome: Import validates required components; roles are installed; version is visible; unsupported dependencies fail clearly.
Risk: Managed-solution dependency/import drift

Epic: E01 - Product foundation and ALM
Title: As a solution architect, I want CCSB's physical Dataverse data model, keys, relationships, ownership, lifecycle and retention controls implemented so that the product has a supportable, upgrade-safe data foundation.
AcceptanceOutcome: The managed solution contains documented ccsb_* tables, relationships, keys, choice/status lifecycles, security/ownership design, migration/seed strategy and validation for the optional native schedule graph.
Risk: Incorrect physical model or destructive schema evolution
Traceability: DM §6; FR-GPH-001; FR-CFG-002/003; FR-ALM-001; NFR-MNT-001; NFR-ALM-002

Epic: E01 - Product foundation and ALM
Title: As a release manager, I want configuration schema and product compatibility checks so that incompatible configurations do not activate after an upgrade.
AcceptanceOutcome: Validation detects incompatible schema; activation is blocked; diagnostics identify migration action.
Risk: Configuration becomes incompatible after upgrade

Epic: E01 - Product foundation and ALM
Title: As a product owner, I want an environment-level board registry so that each Dataverse environment is an isolated CCSB tenant.
AcceptanceOutcome: Board keys unique per environment; no cross-environment lookup/administration path exists.
Risk: Board/tenant isolation or permission leakage

Epic: E02 - Board configuration and runtime projection
Title: As a board administrator, I want to create and clone board versions so that configuration changes do not alter an active board unexpectedly.
AcceptanceOutcome: Active version cannot be edited; clone begins as Draft; compare shows changes.
Risk: Active configuration is mutated or lifecycle bypassed

Epic: E02 - Board configuration and runtime projection
Title: As a board administrator, I want to map Booking, Activity, Assignment and Resource entities so that CCSB can use customer-owned Dataverse data.
AcceptanceOutcome: Metadata selectors validate tables/relationships; missing mandatory mapping blocks activation.
Risk: Unsupported customer schema creates bespoke implementation

Epic: E02 - Board configuration and runtime projection
Title: As a board administrator, I want to map write and rollback-managed fields separately so that CCSB changes only approved scheduling data.
AcceptanceOutcome: Write and rollback flags independently stored; rollback cannot affect unmapped/unmanaged fields.
Risk: Rollback overwrites non-managed customer data

Epic: E02 - Board configuration and runtime projection
Title: As a board administrator, I want generated read-only runtime projection JSON so that runtime loading is efficient without creating a second editable configuration source.
AcceptanceOutcome: Projection source hash/version created after validation; manual JSON edit is not offered; stale projection blocks runtime.
Risk: Stale projection causes runtime/configuration mismatch

Epic: E02 - Board configuration and runtime projection
Title: As a Configuration Administrator, I want a guided Planora Configuration Studio PCF so that I can create, edit, validate, preview, version and activate Schedule Board configurations without manually maintaining interdependent ccsb_* records.
AcceptanceOutcome: A Configuration Administrator can author draft versions through metadata-aware screens; active versions remain immutable; configuration validation, projection generation and activation use governed APIs; model-driven forms remain the support and low-level administration fallback.
Risk: Configuration PCF duplicates or bypasses the authoritative configuration lifecycle, metadata validation or security boundary
Traceability: FR-CFG-001..010; FR-SEC-001/002; FR-API-001; NFR-UX-001/002; NFR-MNT-001; NFR-ALM-001

Epic: E03 - Resource, grouping and capacity configuration
Title: As a board administrator, I want to define one to five resource grouping levels so that dispatchers can view the operating hierarchy.
AcceptanceOutcome: Direct field, lookup path and governed resolver options; levels are ordered; fallback label supported.
Risk: Grouping complexity or unsafe resolver behavior

Epic: E03 - Resource, grouping and capacity configuration
Title: As a board administrator, I want each resource to resolve to one stable group path so that events and capacity do not duplicate across branches.
AcceptanceOutcome: Validation detects ambiguous/multiple paths; runtime shows a single path per board version.
Risk: Duplicate resource paths distort visibility/capacity

Epic: E03 - Resource, grouping and capacity configuration
Title: As a board administrator, I want named resource roles and requirements so that group bookings can demand the right people, vehicles, rooms or equipment.
AcceptanceOutcome: Roles can restrict type/capability; requirement stores quantity/capacity; validation supports partial fill.
Risk: Incorrect role/capacity calculation compromises operations

Epic: E04 - Runtime workspace and read model
Title: As a scheduler, I want the Planora Schedule Board Runtime PCF and Projection Controller to resolve an active, validated board projection, compose the operational workspace and route all actions through governed APIs so that the board is fast, safe and consistent.
AcceptanceOutcome: The Runtime PCF loads only active validated compatible projections, fails closed for missing/stale/incompatible projection, performs visible-slice reads, routes all mutations through Custom APIs, refreshes only affected scope and does not directly write schedule, configuration or control records.
Risk: Runtime PCF becomes an ungoverned second business-logic layer or presents stale data after a command

Epic: E04 - Runtime workspace and read model
Title: As a scheduler, I want to select a permitted board and date range so that I can work in the correct operational context.
AcceptanceOutcome: Only allowed boards show; active validated version loads; date navigation uses board timezone.
Risk: Invalid board context or timezone misinterpretation

Epic: E04 - Runtime workspace and read model
Title: As a scheduler, I want a virtualised group/resource timeline with server paging so that large schedules remain usable.
AcceptanceOutcome: Only current page/viewport data loaded; request cancellation works; performance baseline met.
Risk: Dense schedule performance and stale client data

Epic: E04 - Runtime workspace and read model
Title: As a scheduler, I want search, filters and saved non-sensitive preferences so that I can repeatedly focus on my work.
AcceptanceOutcome: Filter changes refresh correct slice; preference respects permissions/policy.
Risk: Unsafe filters/preferences leak or corrupt user context

Epic: E04 - Runtime workspace and read model
Title: As a scheduler, I want overlays for availability, leave, work hours and holidays so that I can make safe scheduling choices.
AcceptanceOutcome: Configured overlays appear; missing mappings degrade safely; non-colour cues provided.
Risk: Incorrect overlay time interpretation creates unsafe choices

Epic: E05 - Schedule graph operations
Title: As a scheduler, I want to create or edit a booking/activity with its own time so that I can schedule the actual work rather than only a parent record.
AcceptanceOutcome: Activity start/end independent; invalid interval rejected; parent context retained.
Risk: Customer data mutation rules are incomplete

Epic: E05 - Schedule graph operations
Title: As a scheduler, I want to assign one or more resources to an activity with roles so that group work is represented accurately.
AcceptanceOutcome: One activity supports multiple assignments; role compatibility validated; parent/sibling context visible.
Risk: Invalid resource allocation or duplicate assignment

Epic: E05 - Schedule graph operations
Title: As a scheduler, I want to move, resize and reassign activities/assignments so that schedule changes are controlled and explained.
AcceptanceOutcome: Validate first; warn/confirm; execute with row version; affected scope refreshes.
Risk: Non-atomic move/resize/reassign or concurrency loss

Epic: E05 - Schedule graph operations
Title: As a scheduler, I want an assignment time override when the board permits it so that a resource can be scheduled for a portion of the activity.
AcceptanceOutcome: Override only appears when configured; rules enforce allowed boundary; inherited time remains default.
Risk: Override semantics diverge from activity schedule

Epic: E05 - Schedule graph operations
Title: As a scheduler, I want distinct booking, activity and assignment status transitions so that operational state is accurate at the right level.
AcceptanceOutcome: Independent configured transitions; forbidden transitions rejected; history audited.
Risk: Status transition bypass or incorrect side effect

Epic: E06 - Requirements, conflicts and unscheduled work
Title: As a scheduler, I want required versus allocated quantity/capacity for an activity so that I can see whether group work is complete.
AcceptanceOutcome: Eligible assignment statuses count correctly; shortfall/over-allocation displayed with text/icon.
Risk: Incorrect capacity/quantity state

Epic: E06 - Requirements, conflicts and unscheduled work
Title: As a scheduler, I want blocking and warning conflicts explained before commit so that I can decide safely.
AcceptanceOutcome: Rule, severity, scope and message returned; warning needs confirmation when configured.
Risk: Rules are bypassed or not explainable

Epic: E06 - Requirements, conflicts and unscheduled work
Title: As a scheduler, I want a conflict workbench so that I can triage unresolved issues without losing timeline context.
AcceptanceOutcome: Filter/sort/select; history retained; actions permission controlled.
Risk: Conflict history/action permissions are incorrect

Epic: E06 - Requirements, conflicts and unscheduled work
Title: As a scheduler, I want an unscheduled/partial workbench so that I can fill unassigned activities and incomplete requirements.
AcceptanceOutcome: Shows configured unassigned/partial work; open/assign actions available.
Risk: Unscheduled work becomes stale or unauthorised

Epic: E07 - Publish, locks and rollback
Title: As a publish manager, I want to preview the resolved publish scope and blockers so that I do not publish an unsafe schedule.
AcceptanceOutcome: Scope, counts, warning/block, pending changes and lock policy displayed; server revalidates.
Risk: Publish preview differs from actual scope

Epic: E07 - Publish, locks and rollback
Title: As a publish manager, I want a successful publish to create immutable snapshots and locks so that official schedules are protected.
AcceptanceOutcome: Header/items created; managed fields included; lock rules enforced; audit linked.
Risk: Official schedule state is partially committed or mutable

Epic: E07 - Publish, locks and rollback
Title: As a publish manager, I want governed rollback to a compatible prior snapshot so that I can recover the published schedule without overwriting unrelated data.
AcceptanceOutcome: Reason/permission required; only rollback-managed fields restored; new audit/result snapshot created.
Risk: Rollback restores wrong scope or fields

Epic: E07 - Publish, locks and rollback
Title: As a scheduler, I want lock state and pending-change state visible so that I understand why a change is blocked or not yet official.
AcceptanceOutcome: Timeline/details show lock/pending badges and text; policy displayed.
Risk: Lock state is ambiguous or stale in UI

Epic: E08 - Security, APIs, audit and support
Title: As a security administrator, I want CCSB action permissions layered below Dataverse access so that the product does not overgrant customer data.
AcceptanceOutcome: Permission cannot bypass Dataverse security; negative role tests pass.
Risk: CCSB overgrants access beyond Dataverse

Epic: E08 - Security, APIs, audit and support
Title: As an engineer, I want versioned validation/execute/publish/rollback Custom APIs so that all schedule mutations use a stable server contract.
AcceptanceOutcome: No direct client mutation; request/response/negative tests; contracts documented.
Risk: API allows direct/unvalidated mutation

Epic: E08 - Security, APIs, audit and support
Title: As a support analyst, I want correlation across UI, operation, audit, conflict and snapshot records so that I can diagnose an outcome end to end.
AcceptanceOutcome: Correlation query returns linked evidence without raw sensitive telemetry.
Risk: Insufficient traceability or sensitive telemetry

Epic: E09 - Quality and release gates
Title: As a QA lead, I want performance tests at the reference profile so that the scheduler is released with measured scale evidence.
AcceptanceOutcome: Initial load, slice refresh, validation and interaction targets measured at 20 concurrent users.
Risk: Unproven scale/performance threshold

Epic: E09 - Quality and release gates
Title: As an accessibility specialist, I want task-based keyboard and assistive-technology validation so that all core scheduling functions are usable without drag/drop.
AcceptanceOutcome: Focused tests cover selection, forms, commands, errors, publish and rollback.
Risk: Pointer-only or inaccessible custom timeline controls

Epic: E09 - Quality and release gates
Title: As a release manager, I want an upgrade rehearsal and support runbook so that configuration and operational issues are recoverable.
AcceptanceOutcome: Managed import/compatibility/rollback rehearsed; support workflows signed off.
Risk: Upgrade/support recovery is untested

Epic: E10 - Deferred direction
Title: As a scheduler, I want split/merge operations so that work can be decomposed or combined with governed graph semantics.
AcceptanceOutcome: Not V1. Requires design approval for event graph, audit, publish and rollback semantics.
Risk: Graph/publish semantics unsupported

Epic: E10 - Deferred direction
Title: As a mobile user, I want offline scheduling so that I can work without connectivity.
AcceptanceOutcome: Not V1. Requires mobile/offline profile, synchronization, conflict policy and dedicated NFR baseline.
Risk: Offline sync and conflict data loss

Epic: E10 - Deferred direction
Title: As an integration owner, I want outbound connectors so that schedule changes can inform other platforms.
AcceptanceOutcome: Not V1. Requires outbox delivery, retries, idempotency, security and monitoring design.
Risk: Connector failures affect transaction integrity

Epic: E10 - Deferred direction
Title: As an enterprise user, I want URS/Field Service/Project Operations adapters so that selected data can interoperate.
AcceptanceOutcome: Not V1. Requires licensed contract, regression and support matrix.
Risk: Licensing/model drift makes adapter unmaintainable
